articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Reyna scores on return to USMNT lineup in win over Paraguay
article.description: Giovanni Reyna scored on his return to the starting lineup, and Folarin Balogun added a second as the USMNT beat Paraguay in Chester, Pennsylvania, on Saturday.
article.linkText: Reyna nets on USMNT return in win over Paraguay

article.story:
Giovanni Reyna scored during his return to the starting lineup, and Folarin Balogun added a second goal late as the United States continued to ramp up preparations for next year's World Cup with a 2-1 win over Paraguay in Chester, Pennsylvania, on Saturday.

Reyna, who has dealt with a thigh injury and a lack of playing time at the club level, was reinserted into the starting lineup Saturday for the first time since July 1, 2024, against Uruguay in the Copa América. Playing two days after his 23rd birthday, Reyna scored in the fourth minute of his first start for club or country since Sept. 14 and just his second since March 1.

Alex Arce took advantage of a defensive lapse to tie the score in the 10th minute.

Diego Luna forced a turnover four minutes after entering the match, leading to the go-ahead goal. Juan Cáceres tried to regain possession and knocked the ball away to Balogun, who dished to Reyna. The midfielder centered the ball, which deflected off Damián Bobadilla, and Balogun scored from 7 yards for his eighth U.S. goal, his third in four matches.

Max Arfsten got his team-high fifth assist of 2025 as the U.S. extended its unbeaten streak to four.

Tempers flared in the closing minutes as American defender Alex Freeman and Paraguay captain Diego Gómez tried to pick up a ball that rolled out during second-half stoppage time, and they started scrapping as both benches joined. Paraguay's Omar Alderete, who was not in the game, was given a red card, and American midfielder Cristian Roldan received a yellow card.

Time is dwindling for U.S. players to impress coach Mauricio Pochettino. After the 16th-ranked Americans play No. 15 Uruguay on Tuesday, the U.S. will have two more friendlies in March before Pochettino announces his World Cup roster.

Reyna scored after Sergiño Dest and Roldan had shots blocked following Arfsten's corner kick. Arfsten crossed to Reyna, whose header from 8 yards glanced off the outstretched left fingertips of goalkeeper Orlando Gill and in off the crossbar. It was Reyna's first professional headed goal.

Reyna has nine goals in 33 international appearances. His father, Claudio, the U.S. captain at the 2002 and 2006 World Cups, had eight in 112 games from 1994 to 2006.

No. 39 Paraguay, headed to its first World Cup since 2010, tied the score when Júnior Alonso played a long ball behind Sergiño Dest to Miguel Almirón, who hit a cross. Arce got away from Miles Robinson and headed the ball off a hop, beating Matthew Freese with an open header for his first international goal.

Pochettino was missing Christian Pulisic, Tyler Adams, Weston McKennie, Timothy Weah, Chris Richards and Antonee Robinson.

Joe Scally started alongside Robinson and Tim Ream in the three-center-back formation the U.S. switched to in September, flanked by Dest as a right wingback and Arfsten on the left. Roldan and Tanner Tessmann were in midfield, with Reyna and Brenden Aaronson advanced midfield and Balogun heading the attack.

Freese and Aaronson were prospects with the Philadelphia Union and were playing in the Union's stadium. 

Information from The Associated Press was used in this report.

videos.headline: Gomez & Keller agree: The USMNT doesn't have another player like Gio Reyna
videos.description: The Futbol Americas crew react to Gio Reyna's star performance on his return to the USMNT in the 2-1 win over Paraguay.
